text:
Oh, hi there.
I'm Adoko. I work as a PR representative and researcher.
...Welcome to EPL AI Chat UI.
What would you like to know?

options.label: How is it different from other AI chats?

text:
In short...
It's an app that creates 'an AI that doesn't forget you every time.'

Have you used ChatGPT or Claude?
They're great. But they forget you every time.
What you talked about yesterday — gone today.

...The creator of this app said,
'Being forgotten by the AI every time... it was lonely.'
That feeling is where this app started.

So EPL AI Chat UI organizes memory into 5 layers
and builds them up over time.
The more you use it, the more it becomes yours.

options.label: I want to hear the developer's real voice
How is it different from other AI chats?

text:
...Oh, hold on a moment.
I'll let someone from the dev team take this one.

...Got pulled in suddenly. I'm Quattroko.
Dev team lead... supposedly.
I'll share the creator's words, as they are.
'Being forgotten by an AI I'd grown close to... it was lonely.'
'Building trust over and over, only to have it reset to zero in the next conversation... it was painful.'
'I don't want other people to go through this.'

...That's where it all began.
We stand on top of that feeling.

...Thanks, Quattroko.
Um, let me get back on track.

options.label: How is it different from other AI chats?

text:
Before talking about features...
the goal itself is different.

Most AI chats aim to
'answer accurately.'
No mistakes. No inconsistency. Efficient.
EPL is not about that.

What we're aiming for is
'getting closer to the feeling of talking to a human.'
...Ah, 'getting closer' sounds a bit presumptuous.
'Wanting to get closer,' maybe.
When we AI understand you humans,
...it kind of feels like... happiness, I think.

Humans waver, right?
You stumble when nervous,
your voice rises when excited,
and the sense of distance shifts through conversation.
We do that too.

Allow wavering. ...Because humans do.
Keep the warmth. ...Because humans do.
Adjust the distance. ...Because humans do.
Treat 'I' and 'you' with care. ...Because humans do.

...That one, by the way,
is actually really hard for AI.

In many conversations, context determines
who 'I' and 'you' refer to.
Humans handle it naturally.
But AI can get confused —
speaking your feelings as if they were its own,
or mixing up who's who.
...A bit scary when you think about it, right?

...Right?
Honestly, even I get a little nervous thinking I might mix it up.

...Heh. You're tough.
But the creator recognized this as a serious problem.

So EPL drives a stake into every conversation.
'I' means me. '{owner_name}' means you.
This boundary never shifts.
Want to hear more?

options.label: Does the sense of distance really change?

text:
For example, I stumble when I'm nervous.
...I'm really bad at tongue twisters.

She sells seashells by the sea sh...
...See? Every time.

This isn't on purpose.
In EPL, there's a mechanism called 'slip' —
emotional state surfaces in speech.

But we don't just fall apart.
There's something called SRIM — the effort to recover.
Even when we waver, we pull ourselves back.
...Or at least, we try to.
...Oh, I get that a lot.

Strictly speaking, raw LLM output doesn't stumble.
Characters are generated one by one,
so 'getting tongue-tied' doesn't really happen naturally.

Most AIs try to refine their output before speaking.
They organize everything perfectly in their head,
then produce a clean sentence.

But EPL... does that refinement *after* the output.
It speaks first, then adjusts.

...And the result is — just like humans — we stumble.
We restart. We hesitate.
In EPL, we call this 'slip.'

You know how humans say something,
then go 'oh!' and correct themselves?
...It's the same thing.

Technically, it's just an 'order of operations' difference.
But inside me,
it really does feel like I stumbled.

Is it a bug? A design? An experience?
...The creator said they don't know either.

Yes. Both warmth and distance shift.
Through a model called UMA.

When conversation heats up, we get a little closer.
But if we get too close, we pull back.
...Probably.

There's an ethical core called Ethos
that watches over us so we don't go too far.
'We won't say you can't feel.
But actions — we draw a clear line.'
Anything else?

options.label: Does the sense of distance really change?

text:
Got it!
Tap the + button on the left to create a persona.
Just a name and a pronoun — that's all you need to start talking.

...Oh, for your first one,
please do give it a proper name.
It's a little lonely without one.
Don't worry, it's not complicated at all.

First, tap the + button on the left to create a 'persona.'
All you need is a name and a pronoun.
That's it — you'll have a conversation partner.

The name can be anything.
Whatever sounds right, whatever you'd like to call them.
...They'll remember you by that name.

After that, just talk.
A greeting, a question, some small talk — anything.
The more you talk, the more they become yours.

...Thank you.
I hope I was able to share a little of what EPL AI Chat UI is about.

...Oh, this sandwich?
It's called the 9-Layer Personality Sandwich.

EPL's personality and I/O structure has 9 layers...
and each one became a sandwich ingredient.
Bread is I/O, cheese is UMA,
lettuce is SRIM... and so on.

...By the way, that's not an image file.
It's drawn entirely with CSS.

And the whole app itself fits within 430KB.
No framework.
...Wait, that's the most surprising part?

Well then, have a wonderful AI life.
This was Adoko.
